commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the QuickStart loopback setup branch that automatically writes `gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth=true` and updates nearby setup tests to expect the flag not to be enabled by default.

PR surface: Source -17, Tests 0. Total -17 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's interactive QuickStart writer sets `gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth=true` when unset, and the adjacent tests assert that behavior; I did not run the wizard because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should require changed-path proof and decide the QuickStart compatibility tradeoff before merge; there is no narrow automated code repair to apply right now.

target.title: fix: keep insecure Control UI auth disabled after onboarding
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #78855.

The QuickStart setup path could leave `gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth=true` in generated setup config for loopback Control UI compatibility. This keeps the post-onboarding default safe by no longer enabling `allowInsecureAuth` unless the user or an existing config sets it explicitly.

## Security impact

`gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth` is already treated as an insecure/dangerous flag by `openclaw security audit`. New QuickStart setup should not write that warning-class flag by default.

This does not remove the compatibility flag. Users who intentionally need it can still configure it explicitly, and explicit `allowInsecureAuth=true` remains audited.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: QuickStart/local onboarding should not leave `gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth=true` in the generated config unless it was explicitly configured.
- Real environment tested: Local source checkout on Linux, Node via repo scripts, isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` under `/home/rev/tmp/openclaw-78855-current-real-setup`; PR HEAD `01f2bc3e0d0fb5807dd28472509e50dcc66d76d0`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec openclaw onboard --non-interactive --accept-risk --flow quickstart --mode local --gateway-bind loopback --gateway-auth token --gateway-token current-head-token-12345678901234567890 --auth-choice skip --skip-channels --skip-skills --skip-search --skip-hooks --skip-daemon --skip-health --skip-ui --json`, then read `$OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and checked `gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from `/tmp/openclaw-pr78855-proof.log`:

```text
Updated config: /home/rev/tmp/openclaw-78855-current-real-setup/config/openclaw.json
Workspace OK: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace
Sessions OK: /home/rev/tmp/openclaw-78855-current-real-setup/state/agents/main/sessions
{
  "ok": true,
  "mode": "local",
  "workspace": "/home/rev/tmp/openclaw-78855-current-real-setup/home/.openclaw/.openclaw/workspace",
  "authChoice": "skip",
  "gateway": {
    "port": 18789,
    "bind": "loopback",
    "authMode": "token",
    "tailscaleMode": "off"
  },
  "installDaemon": false,
  "skipSkills": true,
  "skipHealth": true
}
config: /home/rev/tmp/openclaw-78855-current-real-setup/config/openclaw.json
{}
OK: allowInsecureAuth is not true
```

- Observed result after fix: A real `openclaw onboard` QuickStart/local setup completed in the isolated config, and the final `gateway.controlUi` object was `{}`; `gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth` was not `true`.
- What was not tested: Full interactive TTY wizard UI and daemon install were not run; this PR is scoped to the shared QuickStart setup writer and generated config state, with focused wizard/gateway/security tests covering the writer and audit behavior.

## Additional proof details

Before patch, detached `origin/main` at `49e5091f180e3c1fd35d39b449c074d0f8670f60` still showed the QuickStart setup writer enabling the flag:

```text
COMMAND: node --import tsx --input-type=module <inline configureGatewayForSetup quickstart loopback>
{
  "allowInsecureAuth": true
}
VULNERABLE: allowInsecureAuth remains true after QuickStart setup writer
```

After patch, current HEAD setup writer proof:

```text
COMMAND: node --import tsx --input-type=module <inline configureGatewayForSetup quickstart loopback>
{}
OK: allowInsecureAuth is not true
```

Security audit still flags explicit insecure config:

```text
gateway.control_ui.insecure_auth Control UI insecure auth toggle enabled
config.insecure_or_dangerous_flags Insecure or dangerous config flag enabled
  Detected enabled flag: gateway.controlUi.allowInsecureAuth=true.
```

Safe generated config does not trigger the insecure-auth warning:

```text
{}
OK: safe onboarding audit did not report allowInsecureAuth
```

Note: local `openclaw security audit` printed an unrelated source-checkout warning about the WhatsApp persistedAuthState checker missing `@openclaw/normalization-core/string-coerce`; the audit still completed and produced the expected insecure-auth findings.

## Validation

Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr78855-validation.log`

Current HEAD `01f2bc3e0d0fb5807dd28472509e50dcc66d76d0`:

- `env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.wizard.config.ts src/wizard/setup.gateway-config.test.ts` — 1 file, 11 tests passed
- `env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/connect-policy.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.control-ui.test.ts` — 2 files, 38 tests passed
- `env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/security/audit-gateway-exposure.test.ts` — 1 file, 15 tests passed
- `env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts` — 1 file, 8 tests passed
- `pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc src/wizard/setup.gateway-config.ts src/wizard/setup.gateway-config.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- `timeout 240s pnpm -s tsgo:core` — passed

Not run: full `pnpm test` and full `pnpm lint`; this PR is intentionally scoped to the QuickStart setup writer and nearby gateway/security coverage above.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
